objectives: Perform comprehensive security audit of mx20022 Rust ISO 20022 library
Check OWASP Top 10 compliance for XML processing and file handling
Identify vulnerabilities in input validation, file handling, and dependency chain
constraints: Library crate (no auth/network surface), CLI tool is primary attack surface
unsafe_code = forbid at workspace level (no unsafe Rust)
important_facts: quick-xml 0.37.5 does NOT process DTDs or external entities (safe by default)
unsafe_code is forbidden workspace-wide via lints
cargo audit reports 0 known vulnerabilities in 131 crate dependencies
validate and translate commands enforce 10MB file size limit
inspect and codegen commands have NO file size limit
xml_scan module uses naive string scanning instead of proper XML parsing
Pattern validation in error messages echoes user input verbatim
FedNow amount validation uses f64 for max_amount boundary, risking precision
risks_issues: MEDIUM: inspect and codegen CLI commands lack file size limits (DoS vector)
LOW: xml_scan string scanning can be confused by XML comments/CDATA
LOW: Error messages echo raw user input values (minor info leak)
INFO: f64 usage in FedNow amount boundary comparison

iteration.summary: Completed full security audit of the mx20022 Rust ISO 20022 library. Reviewed all source files in mx20022-parse, mx20022-validate, mx20022-cli, and mx20022-codegen. Checked dependency chain via cargo audit (clean). Identified 2 medium findings, 3 low findings, and several informational items. The codebase has strong security fundamentals: unsafe_code is forbidden, no DTD/XXE exposure, file size guards on most CLI commands, proper error types, and good dependency governance via deny.toml.
next_actions: Optionally fix identified issues per severity priority
